verdict: 2026-07-10 08:52:59 PDT - Not fully sound yet. The README diagram, multiplier removal, operator-decryptable disclosure, and privacy/support copy fixes are directionally resolved. The two-backlog approach is acceptable in principle because it preserves the roadmap deep links, but the roadmap still overclaims that its execution plan inventories every open unit of work while omitting the root BACKLOG BUG/OPS queue. I also found remaining per-person-only wording in canonical/public docs and one stale Support effective date.

findings.title: Canonical/public docs still define the input as per-person-only
findings.claim: The sweep neutralized README, Privacy, and Support, but the canonical rate note still says the rate is what you enter "for each person," Documentation opens with "The figure you enter for each person," and Terms describes entered values as each person's alternative work. That conflicts with the shipped Simple default, where the ordinary input is one average rate plus participant count and non-hosts cannot use per-person mode.
findings.alternative: Rewrite the canonical definition and public docs around "the rate(s) you enter, whether an average in Simple mode or per-person values in host/co-host per-person mode," and make the default-rate tip explicitly per-person-only.
findings.win: Removes the remaining stale model from the source-of-truth concept doc and the public docs users/legal reviewers read, so future copy does not re-import the old per-person-first framing.

findings.title: Backlog reconciliation leaves the execution-plan authority claim too broad
findings.claim: Naming both backlogs is a reasonable shape, but the roadmap's Execution plan still says it is the single authoritative answer and inventories every open unit of work from reviews/backlog.md, while BUG-1/OPS-1/AUDIT-4 live only in root BACKLOG.md. reviews/backlog.md also says the roadmap is the authoritative ordered inventory of every open item below. The result is better discoverability than before, but still a source-of-truth overclaim for open work ordering.
findings.alternative: Either add a small root-BACKLOG workflow queue row/section to the execution plan, or narrow the execution-plan language to product/strategy work and explicitly say AUDIT/BUG/OPS workflow items are tracked separately in BACKLOG.md without roadmap ordering.
findings.win: Keeps the deep links intact while eliminating the false "every open unit" claim and making the deferred BUG/OPS work visible from the place readers use for sequencing.

findings.title: Support page effective date stayed stale after substantive copy changes
findings.claim: Support now explains the Simple default and operator-decryptable storage posture, but both its header and footer still say Effective date: 1 June 2026. That misses the sweep's date-consistency nit for pages whose content changed.
findings.alternative: Bump Support's header/footer effective date to 9 July 2026, or change non-policy pages to a clearly maintained "Last updated" convention and update it here.
findings.win: Makes the public docs' dates match actual changed content without touching legal terms that only received nav changes.
